mtc_cueing_setup_types.name: Event Start points with additional info.
Event Stop points with additional info.

special_subtypes.name: Special refers to the set-up information that affects a unit globally (as opposed to
special_subtypes.description: individual tracks, sounds, programs, sequences, etc.). In this case, the Special Type takes the place of the Event Number. Five are defined. Note that types 01 00 through 04 00 ignore the event time field.

special_subtypes.name: Time Code Offset refers to a relative Time Code offset for each unit. For
special_subtypes.description: example, a piece of video and a piece of music that are supposed to go together may be created at different times, and more than likely have different absolute time code positions - therefore, one must be offset from the other so that they will match up. Just like there is one master time code for an entire system, each unit only needs one offset value per unit.

special_subtypes.name: Enable Event List means for a unit to enable execution of events in its
special_subtypes.description: list if the appropriate MTC or SMPTE time occurs.

special_subtypes.name: Disable Event List means for a unit to disable execution of its event list
special_subtypes.description: but not to erase it. This facilitates an MTC Event Manager in muting particular devices in order to concentrate on others in a complex system where many events occur simultaneously.

special_subtypes.name: Clear Event List means for a unit to erase its entire event list.
System Stop refers to a time when the unit may shut down. This serves
special_subtypes.description: as a protection against Event Starts without matching Event Stops, tape machines running past the end of the reel, and so on.

special_subtypes.name: Event List Request is sent by a master to an MTC peripheral. If the
special_subtypes.description: device 	ID 	(Channel 	Number) 	matches 	that 	of 	the 	peripheral, 	the peripheral responds by transmitting its entire cue list as a sequence of Set Up Messages, starting from the SMPTE time indicated in the Event List Request message.

special_subtypes.name: Punch In and Punch Out refer to the enabling and disabling of record mode on a
special_subtypes.description: unit. The Event Number refers to the track to be recorded. Multiple punch in/punch out points (and any of the other event types below) may be specified by sending multiple Set-Up messages with different times.

special_subtypes.name: Delete Punch In or Out deletes the matching point (time and event number)
Event Start and Stop refer to the running or playback of an event, and imply
special_subtypes.description: that a large sequence of events or a continuous event is to be started or stopped. The event number refers to which event on the targeted device is to be played. A single event (i.e. playback of a specific sample, a fader movement on an automated console, etc.) may occur several times throughout a given list of cues. These events will be represented by the same event number, with different Start and Stop times.

special_subtypes.name: Event Start and Stop with Additional Information refer to an event (as above)
special_subtypes.description: with additional parameters transmitted in the Set Up message between the Time and EOX. The additional parameters may take the form of an effects unit's internal parameters, the volume level of a sound effect, etc. See below for a description of additional information.

special_subtypes.name: Delete Event Start/Stop means to delete the matching (event number and time)
special_subtypes.description: event (with or without additional information) from the Cue List.

special_subtypes.name: Cue Point refers to individual event occurrences, such as marking "hit" points for
special_subtypes.description: sound effects, reference points for editing, and so on. Each Cue number may be assigned to a specific reaction, such as a specific one-shot sound event (as opposed to a continuous event, which is handled by Start/Stop). A single cue may occur several times throughout a given list of cues. These events will be represented by the same event number, with different Start and Stop times.

special_subtypes.name: Cue Point with Additional Information is exactly like Event Start/Stop with
special_subtypes.description: Additional Information, except that the event represents a Cue Point rather than a Start/Stop Point.

special_subtypes.name: Delete Cue Point means to Delete the matching (event number and time) Cue
special_subtypes.description: Event with or without additional information from the Cue List.

special_subtypes.name: Event Name in Additional Information. This merely assigns a name to a given
special_subtypes.description: event number. It is for human logging purposes. See Additional Information description. EVENT TIME: 	This is the SMPTE/MIDI Time Code time at which the given event is supposed to occur. Actual time is in 1/100th frame resolution, for those units capable of handling bits or some other form of sub-frame resolution, and should otherwise be selfexplanatory. EVENT NUMBER: 	This is a fourteen-bit value, enabling 16,384 of each of the above types to be individually addressed. "sl" is the 7 LS bits, and "sm" is the 7 MS bits. ADDITIONAL 	Additional information consists of a nibblized MIDI data stream, INFORMATION: 	LS nibble first. The exception is Set-Up Type OE, where the additional information is nibblized ASCII, LS nibble first. An ASCII newline is accomplished by sending CR and LF in the ASCII. CR alone functions solely as a carriage return, and LF alone functions solely as a Line-Feed. For example, a MIDI Note On message such as 91 46 7F would be nibblized and sent as 01 09 	06 04 0F 07. In this way, any device can decode any message regardless of who it was intended for. Device-specific messages should be sent as nibblized MIDI System Exclusive messages. 10 	MIDI Time Code (Doc 4.2.1)

realtime_cueing_setup_types.name: Event Start points with additional info.
Event Stop points with additional info.

signal_path_modes.description: The Master Time Code Source (tape deck) is in normal PLAY
The Master Time Code Source is being "rocked", or "cued" by hand.
In this mode, the tape is in a high-speed wind or rewind, and is not touching the playback head. No "cueing" of the taped material is
This is just another expression for "Fast-Forward/Rewind Mode".
